original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — recognition, comprehensive assessment, function, and context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.12 — clinician-completed risk assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
intended_setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
